pet.name: Biscuit
pet.special_needs:
Large senior dog with weight management needs, slower walks, joint
  support, and consistent hydration monitoring.
pet.characteristics: Dogs require daily exercise scaled to age and size, consistent training and mental enrichment, regular veterinary checkups, balanced nutrition with portion control, fresh water access, secure shelter, and social interaction. Senior dogs benefit from joint-supportive bedding, gentle movement routines, and close monitoring of hydration, appetite, and mobility. Weight management supports longevity and joint health. Routine dental care, parasite prevention, and age-appropriate screening are important. Environmental comfort, predictable schedules, and pain management support quality of life in later years.
pet.species_profile_summary: Typical companion dog lifespan ranges 10-13 years; breed-specific variation is significant but unknown here, so a mid-range estimate is used for general care planning.

age_context.summary: Typical companion dog lifespan ranges 10-13 years; breed-specific variation is significant but unknown here, so a mid-range estimate is used for general care planning.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Pets on kidney-support diets often need strict consistency around meals and hydration. Wet-food feeding schedules, water refreshes, and appetite monitoring are common routine tasks that owners can track day by day.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning hydration check and water refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Refresh water bowl and observe Biscuit's drinking behavior. Note any changes in intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs require close hydration monitoring. Fresh water access and consistent observation support kidney health and overall wellness.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle low-impact walk or slow movement session
raw_recommendations.notes: Short, slow-paced walk or calm indoor movement. Allow extra time for transitions and rest breaks. Observe gait and mobility.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs benefit from lower-impact exercise scaled to age and size. Shorter walks and joint-friendly movement are easier to tolerate and support joint health without strain.

raw_recommendations.name: Meal feeding with appetite observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed measured portion for weight management. Observe appetite, eating speed, and any changes. Allow slow feeding time.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent meal schedules and portion control support weight management and longevity. Appetite monitoring creates a record of changes that may indicate health shifts.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening hydration check and water refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Refresh water bowl again. Observe drinking and note any patterns or changes in intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second daily hydration check ensures consistent water access and allows tracking of intake patterns, critical for senior dogs with weight and joint concerns.

raw_recommendations.name: Calm indoor enrichment or gentle interaction
raw_recommendations.notes: Sniff walk indoors, gentle brushing, calm play, or quiet interaction. Suitable for rainy days or low-energy periods.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Enrichment combined with observation improves quality of life without requiring intense activity. Short, calm interactions are well-tolerated by senior dogs and support mental wellness.

raw_recommendations.name: Daily bathroom and mobility observation log
raw_recommendations.notes: Record bathroom habits, gait changes, energy level, and any signs of confusion or discomfort. Note any deviations from baseline.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Observation tasks create a health record for special-needs pets. Tracking appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits helps detect early changes and supports veterinary communication.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly joint-support comfort check and bedding adjustment
raw_recommendations.notes: Inspect bedding for adequate cushioning and support. Ensure Biscuit has access to orthopedic or soft bedding. Observe comfort level during rest.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs benefit from joint-supportive bedding and environmental comfort. Regular comfort checks support mobility and reduce pain-related stress.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly weight and appetite trend review
raw_recommendations.notes: Review daily logs for appetite, hydration, and mobility trends. Note any weight changes or behavioral shifts. Prepare summary for veterinarian if needed.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management supports longevity and joint health in senior dogs. Monthly trend review allows early detection of appetite or mobility changes and informs veterinary discussions.
